rank_note: Rank intentionally null. site/src/data/indexes/us-states.json contains only 21 of 51 jurisdictions; ranks 9-38 were lost in the original legacy-HTML extraction and the surviving entries were renumbered contiguously, so the displayed rank column is not a national rank. West Virginia must not be assigned a rank until the index is rebuilt with all 51 jurisdictions.
calibration_note: Not calibrated to the published us-states rank order, which is known to be corrupt. West Virginia is absent from site/src/data/indexes/us-states.json, so there is no prior composite and no placeholder to anchor to. Calibrated to the methodology anchors and to the 17 states assessed at full depth under this methodology: Georgia 26.9, Iowa 30.6, Arizona 36.2, Ohio 38.1, North Carolina 45.0, Alaska 47.5, Oregon 50.6, Colorado 52.5, Wisconsin 53.1, Michigan 53.8, Delaware 53.8, Pennsylvania 54.4, Virginia 55.6, Maryland 55.6, New York 56.9, Illinois 60.0, New Jersey 60.6. West Virginia at 35.6 sits between Iowa and Arizona. Composite verified by running computeCompositeFromDimensions from site/scripts/lib/scoring.mjs (methodology v1.2). Verification output: {"composite":35.6,"band":"Developing","integrationPremium":0}. Inputs AWR 2.6, EMP 2.0, ACT 2.6, EQU 2.4, BND 2.4, ACC 2.4, SYS 2.8, INT 2.2. baseComposite 35.6; stdDev 0.243 so consistencyMult 1.0; 8 of 8 dimensions below 4.0 so weaknessFactor 0 and integrationPremium 0.0; final composite 35.6, band Developing. The composite is 4.4 points from the 40 band boundary, so the band is stable and no instability flag is raised. Three sensitivity cases were also run through computeCompositeFromDimensions rather than computed by hand: raising EMP from 2.0 to 3.0 returns {"composite":38.7,"band":"Developing"}; raising ACC from 2.4 to 3.0 returns {"composite":37.5,"band":"Developing"}; adding 0.5 to every dimension returns {"composite":48.1,"band":"Functional"}. No single-dimension correction moves West Virginia out of the Developing band.
attribution_note: Scores West Virginia state conduct only, across all three branches, 2021-2026, with the past twelve months weighted most heavily. West Virginia had unified Republican control of both political branches for the whole window, with a gubernatorial transition from Jim Justice to Patrick Morrisey in January 2025; both administrations are scored as the same jurisdiction. Federal policy imposed on the state is excluded. Three exclusions matter. First, the 2025 federal reconciliation law changed Medicaid eligibility rules nationally and is not scored against West Virginia; what is scored is the state discretion of having adopted Medicaid expansion effective 2014-01-01 and having kept it. Second, the indefinite federal delay of the coal-mine silica dust rule and the reduction in force at the NIOSH Morgantown campus are federal actions; the black-lung protection that survived came from a private class action by miners and a federal judge, not from the state, and no state objection to either federal action was found, which is scored under S4 Structural Critique. Third, the 199 million dollar Rural Health Transformation award is federal money; what is scored is the plan quality of the state, which drew nearly double the guaranteed first-year minimum, and the delay by the Legislature in authorizing the spending until the final days of the 2026 session. Two premises supplied to the assessor were checked and corrected. There is no settlement in the foster care class action: the case was dismissed in February 2025, revived by the Fourth Circuit in June 2026, and is heading to a bench trial in March 2027. And the West Virginia Legislature did not exempt itself from the Freedom of Information Act: House Bill 3412 passed both chambers in April 2025 but died when the House failed to concur, so no self-exemption is in force.
cross_state_consistency_flag: AB3 Transparency cross-state rule v3: DECLINED - precondition not met. Limb (a) requires either a branch exempting itself from the public-records law or an attack on the oversight body. Neither is established. House Bill 3412 would have removed the Legislature from the definition of a public body, and it passed the House on 2025-04-02 and the Senate on 2025-04-11, but it died when the House of Delegates did not take up the message to concur, so it never became law. The Performance Evaluation and Research Division of the Legislative Auditor has not been narrowed, defunded or stripped of independence protections, and it published a damaging uncompelled finding in January 2026 that the Office of Technology had failed for six years to implement the cybersecurity framework the Legislature mandated in 2019. The weakening the 2025 bill attempted - removing the liberal-construction presumption, extending the response deadline from 5 to 14 days, and adding search and retrieval fees - is a procedural and substantive weakening rather than a structural escape by a branch, which the North Dakota and Idaho refinements say should be scored on the merits rather than collapsed into the cap. AB3 is therefore scored on the merits at 3, not floored at 2 and not capped. Scoring effect: none. AB3 is 3 under the merits analysis and would also be 3 if the cap had been applied, so the composite is 35.6 either way. Applying the floor of 2 instead would give ACC 2.2 and a composite of 34.4.
key_evidence: West Virginia opioid overdose deaths fell 48.55 percent, from 3,969 to an estimated 2,042, comparing the 12 months ending September 2025 with the prior 12 months. That beat the national decline of 44.0 percent over the same period.

West Virginia led the nation in opioid settlement spending reports, with 94 percent of participating local governments filing, and 24.5 percent of the roughly 1 billion dollar settlement goes directly to local governments.

West Virginia bought a 560,000 dollar child-protection intake tool after 14-year-old Kyneddi Miller starved to death in 2024, paid about 223,000 dollars for it, and never put it into use. A state senator on the relevant committee did not know.

A federal magistrate judge found in November 2023 that West Virginia corrections officials intentionally destroyed evidence in a jail-conditions class action, and that the decisions were made by the highest persons in the chain of command, including the Commissioner.
Child Protective Services vacancies fell from 27.3 percent in 2023 to 8.5 percent on 2025-09-01, and turnover fell from more than 34 percent in 2021 to 15 percent in 2025.

The Hope Scholarship voucher opened to all K-12 students in 2026. Lawmakers set aside 277 million dollars for it while enacted Medicaid appropriations were cut 107.1 million dollars below the proposal of the Governor.
Hospitals offering labor and delivery in West Virginia fell from 35 in 2005 to 16 in May 2026. Nearly half the counties of the state are now maternity care deserts.

State employee health premiums rose 14 percent for the 2026 plan year, the spousal surcharge went from 147 to 350 dollars a month, and one 28-year school employee saw premiums rise 190 percent over four years.
Twelve years after the Elk River spill left 300,000 people without safe tap water, delegates voted 58-34 in March 2026 to roll back a layer of the storage-tank rules the spill produced.
The intellectual and developmental disability waiver waitlist reached 1,031 people on 2025-10-07, and 789 of them were children.

evidence.quote: West Virginia led the nation in opioid settlement reporting transparency, achieving a 94% compliance rate among participating jurisdictions
evidence.claim: uncompelled, nation-leading spend reporting; primary support for AB3 Transparency at 3 and S5 Coalitional Compassion at 4

evidence.quote: The state paid roughly $223,000 to nonprofit Evident Change before canceling the contract
evidence.claim: a fatality-driven reform bought and abandoned; drives AB4 Systemic Learning to 2 and I2 Non-Performance to 2

evidence.quote: the intentional decisions to not preserve evidence were not done by low-level employees but were perpetrated by the highest persons in the chain of command including the Commissioner of the WVDCR
evidence.claim: concealment rather than acknowledgment of harm; drives E4 Validation to 1 and AB1 Harm Acknowledgment to 2

evidence.quote: As of September 1, 2025, the statewide vacancy rate for CPS positions stands at 8.5%, down from 12.1% at the start of the year and from 27.3% in 2023
evidence.claim: a real, measured, published frontline workforce recovery; primary support for B1 Self-Sustainability and I5 Resilience of Care

evidence.quote: Hope Scholarship price $155M so far after 26,617 WV students apply
evidence.claim: universal, non-means-tested spending grew while targeted programs were constrained; primary support for EQ2 Priority for Vulnerable at 2

evidence.quote: state general revenue appropriations for Medicaid in the enacted FY 2027 budget were cut by $107.1 million compared to Governor Morrisey initial proposal
evidence.claim: the trade-off that fixes EQ2 and I1; also supports S2 Long-Term Impact at 2

evidence.quote: The number of hospitals offering labor and delivery services in West Virginia dropped from 35 in 2005 to 16 as of May 2026
evidence.claim: a measured, worsening access gap in the past twelve months; primary support for EQ4 Access Design at 2

evidence.quote: A monthly spousal surcharge for state employees will more than double, from $147 to $350
evidence.claim: cost shifted onto the workforce of the state itself; primary support for I3 Internal Consistency at 2

evidence.quote: Delegates are split in vote to roll back a layer of tank regulations put in place after water crisis
evidence.claim: the state unlearning its own most important safety lesson; drives S4 Structural Critique to 1 and EQ5 to 2

evidence.quote: As of October 7, 2025, the IDD waitlist had 1,031 people, with 789 individuals under the age of 18
evidence.claim: need documented and unmet; primary support for AC2 Proportionality at 2

evidence.quote: 522,000 children and adults are enrolled in West Virginia Medicaid, and 34.6 percent of West Virginians are enrolled in Medicaid or CHIP
evidence.claim: the single strongest equity fact about the state; primary support for EQ1 Universality at 4

evidence.quote: Federal judge denies West Virginia request to pause child welfare lawsuit
evidence.claim: the state is litigating rather than settling a systemic constitutional claim; supports I4 Values Alignment at 2

evidence.quote: continued delay in legislative spending authorization for the federally awarded Rural Health Transformation Program puts critical rural health improvements, and future federal funding, at risk
evidence.claim: the health department of the state warning about its own Legislature; primary support for I1 Consistency Under Pressure at 2

evidence.quote: New FOIA bill guts West Virginia presumption of transparency
evidence.claim: the attempted records-law weakening that did not become law; central to the AB3 cross-state rule disposition

evidence.quote: as of 2025, such a program has not been implemented throughout state agencies
evidence.claim: an independently funded oversight body still publishing uncompelled damaging findings; limb (b) of the AB3 rule and support for A3 Blind Spot Mitigation
evidence.source: WV Legislative Auditor, Performance Evaluation and Research Division
